titlestmt.author: Compiled by G. Soloman, M. Warren

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-08-29 18:31:50 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Legal documents, 1790-1897, include articles of agreement, deeds, mortgages, and property titles in and around Ithaca, New York; family material includes photos and correspondence

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: George S. Kephart is the great-grandson of Horace Mack, Sr. and Eliza Ann Ferris, and the grandson of Horace Mack, Jr. and Lucy Wheeler.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: George S. Kephart family papers, #3268. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Legal documents, 1791-1897, include articles of agreement, deeds, mortgages, and property titles in and around Ithaca, New York, drawn up by Horace Mack, Sr. and Jr., Benjamin G. Ferris, and Joshua Ferris. Also included are affidavits of application for military land grants by Revolutionary War soldiers, 1818; maps of Tioga County (New York) township divisions, surveyed, 1808; will of Thomas Hathway bequeathing a portion of land to the Publick Universal Friend (Jemima Wilkinson), 1795; expense account, probably of Benjamin Ferris, for a trip to Albany, 1827; and correspondence between Mack and Ferris family members about banking, legal favors, and property interests. Personal papers include bills and receipts, 1804-1902; a deed for land in Montgomery County, New York for John Shelly signed by Governor George Clinton; a well water analysis, Ithaca, New York, 1895; manuscripts on local history, 1901, 1975; and miscellaneous correspondence. Also, material concerning the Riley and Kephart families. Correspondence of Horace Kephart and printed material pertaining to his work as a naturalist and early advocate for establishing the Great Smoky Mountains National Park.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to George S. Kephart include photograph album/scrapbooks from Bangor, Maine, New York-Minneapolis, Klamath, Oregon, and Chicago-Washington; personal reminiscences; materials about World War I service in France; writings and publications; Cornell Crew materials; and materials about the Kephart Glen in the Cornell plantations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Memorabilia, primarily relating to Leonard W. Kephart (Class of 1913), including postcards, letters, programs, photographs, views of Ithaca, clippings, 1909 high school class prophecy, and a variety of items from his time at Cornell and later. Manuscript of poem: "Trooper [Trumpeter] George" written by Horace Mack, Jan. 22, 1899, illustrated by Julia Mack; other poems and rhymes by Horace Mack.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Items relating to Laura Kephart, including a letter to her cousin (with criticism of Franklin D. Roosevelt) and miscellaneous photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Also inscribed copy of "Courtney: Master Oarsman - Champion Coach" by Margaret K. Look, "The Cornell Navy," by C.V.P. Young, and 1913 Cornell Students' Handbook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Horace Mack's diploma from Hamilton College, 1898

controlaccess.geogname: Great Smoky Mountains National Park (N.C. and Tenn.)
controlaccess.subject: World War, 1914-1918.
Real property -- New York (State) -- Montgomery County.
National parks and reserves -- United States.
Naturalists.
Families -- New York (State)

unittitle.extref: Link to the finding aid. PDF file.

did.unittitle: H. Mack Checks drawn on Ithaca Banks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes receipts for payments of mortgages, debts, assigned judgements, and other legal fees; balances due; and statement of contracts

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous Deeds and Mortgages in and near Ithaca

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mortgage deeds for land in Minnesota; sale of land in Minnesota; Deed of Mining Claim in Montana; tax sale of land in Warren County, PA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents asking Mack to act as proxy at any election of directors at Chemung Canal Bank or any meeting of stockholders. Document asking Mack to handle a real estate transaction.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ithaca Journal and Daily Advertiser; Weekly Argus; Journal of Commerce, Jr.; The Owego Gazette; Ithaca Daily Journal; Weekly Argus and Rough-hewer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Genealogy of Mack, Ferris, Hathaway, Kephart, and Riley families

scopecontent.p: A water analysis of an Ithaca Well, and an unidentified/undated poem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including La Chanson du Soldat Quand Madelon, the most popular song among French soldiers and their allier troops in France in WW I.
